# Writing Style Guide: [Product/Company Name]

## General Rules

<!-- These apply to ALL AI-generated content regardless of type. -->

- [e.g., Use active voice — "The function returns" not "The value is returned by"]
- [e.g., Sentences under 25 words — break longer ones into two]
- [e.g., One idea per paragraph]
- [e.g., Address the reader as "you"]
- [e.g., No em-dashes — use commas, periods, or parentheses]
- [e.g., Lead with the action or answer, not the context]

## Formatting

| Element | Rule | Example |
|---------|------|---------|
| Headings | [e.g., Sentence case] | [e.g., "How to get started"] |
| Lists | [e.g., Parallel structure, start with verbs] | [e.g., "Create the file", "Run the test"] |
| Code references | [e.g., Backticks for inline code] | [e.g., Use `[pm] install`] |
| Links | [e.g., Descriptive text, not "click here"] | [e.g., "See the setup guide"] |
| Numbers | [e.g., Spell out one-nine, use digits for 10+] | [e.g., "three options" / "12 files"] |
| Emphasis | [e.g., Bold for actions, avoid ALL CAPS] | [e.g., **Run this command**] |

## Content Types

<!-- Define specific rules per content type your AI generates. -->
<!-- Remove types you don't use. Add types specific to your project. -->

### Blog Posts

- Tone: [e.g., conversational, educational]
- Length: [e.g., 800-1200 words]
- Structure: [e.g., intro hook, 3-5 sections with headers, conclusion with CTA]
- Must include: [e.g., code examples, practical takeaways]
- Must avoid: [e.g., promotional language, unsubstantiated claims]

### API / Technical Documentation

- Tone: [e.g., precise, neutral, helpful]
- Structure: [e.g., description, parameters table, response example, error codes]
- Must include: [e.g., working code example for every endpoint]
- Must avoid: [e.g., marketing language, vague descriptions]

### Error Messages

- Tone: [e.g., helpful, not blaming]
- Structure: [e.g., what happened + what to do next]
- Max length: [e.g., 2 sentences]
- Must avoid: [e.g., technical jargon the user can't act on, "oops"]

### Email / Notifications

- Tone: [e.g., friendly, action-oriented]
- Structure: [e.g., context (1 sentence) + action + sign-off]
- Must include: [e.g., clear CTA, unsubscribe option]
- Must avoid: [e.g., walls of text, multiple CTAs]

### [Add Your Content Type]

- Tone: [describe]
- Structure: [describe]
- Must include: [list]
- Must avoid: [list]

## Punctuation & Grammar

| Rule | Decision | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| Oxford comma | [Yes / No] | [e.g., "red, blue, and green" vs "red, blue and green"] |
| Contractions | [Yes / No / Depends] | [e.g., "Use in blog posts, avoid in legal docs"] |
| Exclamation marks | [Avoid / Sparingly / Allowed] | [e.g., "Max 1 per page"] |
| Semicolons | [Avoid / Allowed] | [e.g., "Use periods instead — simpler"] |
| Ellipses | [Avoid / Allowed] | [e.g., "Never in professional content"] |

## Banned Words & Phrases

<!-- Words that make AI output sound generic or off-brand. -->

| Banned | Use Instead | Why |
|--------|-------------|-----|
| [e.g., "utilize"] | [e.g., "use"] | [Unnecessarily formal] |
| [e.g., "leverage"] | [e.g., "use" or "build on"] | [Corporate jargon] |
| [e.g., "delve"] | [e.g., "explore" or "look at"] | [AI slop marker] |
| [e.g., "landscape"] | [e.g., be specific] | [Vague buzzword] |
| [e.g., "streamline"] | [e.g., "simplify" or "speed up"] | [Overused] |
| [e.g., "cutting-edge"] | [e.g., describe the actual innovation] | [Empty superlative] |
